summary: Matched three additional Yoshi neutral-special functions in src/melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_SpecialN.c and tightened their header prototypes. The PR replaced placeholder asm markers for fn_8012D298, ftYs_SpecialAirN1_2_Coll, and ftYs_SpecialAirN1_1_Coll with C implementations, yielding 176 newly matched bytes according to the decomp.dev report.
what_changed: Implemented fn_8012D298(Fighter_GObj* gobj), fetching Fighter* with GET_FIGHTER, calling ftCommon_8007D5D4(fp), then calling Fighter_ChangeMotionState(gobj, 0x160, 0x0C4C5092U, fp->cur_anim_frame, 0, ftYs_Init_804D9A38, NULL).
Added an extern declaration for f32 ftYs_Init_804D9A38 in ftYs_SpecialN.c so the motion-state call uses the named float symbol rather than a literal.
Implemented ftYs_SpecialAirN1_2_Coll(ftYs_GObj* gobj) as a thin wrapper around ft_80082C74((Fighter_GObj*) gobj, fn_8012D2F8).
Implemented ftYs_SpecialAirN1_1_Coll(ftYs_GObj* gobj) as a thin wrapper around ft_80082C74((Fighter_GObj*) gobj, fn_8012D360).
Updated ftYs_SpecialN.h prototypes for fn_8012D298, fn_8012D2F8, and fn_8012D360 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete void-returning GObj-typed signatures.
The decomp.dev bot reported 3 new 100% matches: fn_8012D298 (+96 bytes), ftYs_SpecialAirN1_2_Coll (+40 bytes), and ftYs_SpecialAirN1_1_Coll (+40 bytes).
smart_moves: Kept ftYs_Init_804D9A38 as an extern symbol in the argument list to Fighter_ChangeMotionState; the inline source comment notes that replacing it with 0 prevented 100% matching.

Matched the collision callbacks with minimal wrapper bodies instead of over-modeling behavior: both simply cast ftYs_GObj* to Fighter_GObj* and pass the correct continuation callback into ft_80082C74.
Improved header prototypes at the same time as the C matches, reducing unknown signatures and making callback argument types explicit for later nearby work.
decomp_lessons: For matching, a semantic zero literal is not always interchangeable with a global float symbol; preserving the original data reference can be required for codegen identity.
Small fighter collision/state transition functions often decompile as direct helper wrappers. Before attempting complex reconstruction, check whether the body is just a typed cast plus helper call.
When matching one function reveals the real callback signatures of adjacent unresolved functions, update the header prototypes immediately to support cleaner future matches.
Motion-state transition matches may depend on using the current animation frame field, exact flag constants, exact float argument source, and NULL placement in the same call shape.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retained address-based names such as fn_8012D298, fn_8012D2F8, and fn_8012D360.
Yoshi-specific object aliases use ftYs_GObj*, while generic fighter APIs use Fighter_GObj*; wrapper callbacks explicitly cast between them when calling generic helpers.
Yoshi neutral-special collision functions follow the existing ftYs_SpecialAirN1_1_Coll / ftYs_SpecialAirN1_2_Coll naming pattern.
Global data symbol ftYs_Init_804D9A38 follows the character/init address-style naming convention.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use named extern data symbols rather than substituting literals when the original assembly likely loads from data; here ftYs_Init_804D9A38 was needed where 0 did not match.
Preserve exact constants in Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls: action state 0x160 and flags 0x0C4C5092U are evidence-backed from the diff.
Use explicit casts in wrapper calls when the source object typedef differs from the callee's expected GObj type, as in (Fighter_GObj*) gobj passed to ft_80082C74.
Header concretization from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void plus typed GObj parameters can be part of the matching tactic because it documents and constrains callback shapes.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
Automated decomp.dev feedback confirmed 176 newly matched bytes and three new 100% function matches in main/melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_SpecialN.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type fn_8012D298, fn_8012D2F8, or fn_8012D360.
Search for other Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls that require a named global float symbol instead of a literal zero to match.
Look for additional ft_80082C74 collision wrapper patterns in other fighter special move files.
Inspect nearby unresolved placeholders in ftYs_SpecialN.c such as fn_8012D230, fn_8012D3C0, fn_8012D428, and fn_8012D488 for similar wrapper or motion-state structures.
